elements.label: fig

elements.label: sec_1
elements.text:
NIF's India-Japan Fund invests INR 500 cr (US$ 57 m) in EKA Mobility to accelerate India's
transition to sustainable commercial transportation

elements.label: para
elements.text:
India-Japan Fund ("IJP"), a fund managed by National Investment and Infrastructure Fund Limited
("NIIF"), announces an investment of INR 500 cr (US$ 57 mn) in EKA Mobility, a leading electric vehicle
manufacturer focusing on commercial vehicles.

elements.label: para
elements.text:
This aligns with IIF's objective to invest in the climate segment and also further accelerate investments
in the India-Japan corridor.

elements.label: para
elements.text:
EKA Mobility, a subsidiary of Pinnacle Industries, has one of the widest ranges of electric commercial

vehicles developed with in-house R&D and technological capabilities, ably supported by indigenous

software and engineering capabilities.

elements.label: para
elements.text:
The capital infusion from IJF is expected to support EKA Mobility's plans to increase its manufacturing
capacity, advance research and development initiatives, improve its supply chain infrastructure, and
enhance its marketing efforts aimed at domestic and international markets.

elements.label: para
elements.text:
EKA Mobility's existing shareholders include VDL Groep (Netherlands), Mitsui & Co., Ltd. (Japan), and
ENAM Holdings (India).

elements.label: para
elements.text:
Krishna Kumar, Partner & Fund Head-IJF, said, "Our investment in EKA Mobility continues to reflect our
confidence in India's electric mobility potential and the capability of homegrown OEMs to drive
meaningful change in the market. IJF is supporting enterprises that are shaping India's journey to a low-
carbon economy, and also committed to promoting India-Japan business partnerships. We are glad to
support Eka Mobility's growth in the sustainable commercial transportation segment, wherein it
combines its technical, design capabilities and manufacturing infrastructure to take advantage of India's
transition in the mobility segment."

elements.label: para
elements.text:
Makato Uchida, Senior Managing Director at Japan Bank for International Cooperation (JIBC) and
Executive Director at JIBC IG Partners, said,
"We welcome IIF's partnership with EKA Mobility, reflecting
the fund's commitment to invest in opportunities that contribute to environmental preservation in India
as well as to promote collaboration between Indian and Japanese companies. Together with our partner
NIIF, JIBC group -including our subsidiary JIBC IG Partners, which is supporting NIIF in promoting
Japanese investments in India through the IIF initiative - will continue to contribute to deepening the
bilateral relationship between India and Japan.
"

elements.label: para
elements.text:
Dr. Sudhir Mehta, Founder & Chairman of EKA Mobility, added,
"We are proud to have NII as a
strategic partner in our growth journey. This investment comes at a crucial time as we expand
operations, strengthen our R&D and accelerate the adoption of electric mobility across India. NII's
support reinforces our shared vision of creating sustainable, inclusive, and future-ready transportation
solutions.
"

elements.label: fig
elements.text: ![Figure](figures/3PG_PLAIN TEXT_NIIFs-India-Japan-Fund-invests-INR-500-cr-US-57-mn-in-EKA-Mobility_page_002_figure_000.png)

elements.label: fig
elements.text: ![Figure](figures/3PG_PLAIN TEXT_NIIFs-India-Japan-Fund-invests-INR-500-cr-US-57-mn-in-EKA-Mobility_page_002_figure_001.png)

elements.label: para
elements.text:
The collaboration between NIF and EKA Mobility aligns with the Government of India's stated

objectives on electrification of public transport and promotion of domestic manufacturing under the

Make-in-India and Atmanirbarh Bharat initiatives.

elements.label: para
elements.text:
The company's portfolio of zero-emission vehicles spans electric buses (7m, 9m, and 12m variants),

integrity coaches, 3-wheeler passenger and cargo vehicles, heavy-duty trucks (55T and 7T) and small

commercial vehicles in the 1.5T, 2.5T, and 3.5T categories. The company has confirmed orders for over

3,300 electric buses as part of state government procurements, municipal corporation contracts, and

private commercial fleets.

elements.label: sec_1
elements.text: About National Investment and Infrastructure Fund Limited (NIF)

elements.label: para
elements.text:
NIF is India's sovereign-anchored alternative asset manager, catalysing global capital to invest in sectors
and asset classes that play an important role in India's growth journey. Anchored by the Government of
India in partnership with leading global investors, NIF manages USD 4.9 billion in equity capital
commitments across four strategies—Infrastructure, Private Markets, Growth Equity, and Climate
Investments. NIF seeks to deliver superior returns by investing in commercially attractive sectors and
asset classes in India, while generating positive environmental and social impact.

elements.label: para
elements.text:
India-Japan Fund (IJF) is NIF’s first bilateral fund with a corpus of INR 4900 Crore, with the Government
of India (“Gol”) contributing 49% and the remaining 51% contributed by the Japan Bank for International
Cooperation (“JIB”), a policy based financial institution wholly owned by the Government of Japan. IJF
focuses on investing in India’s climate and energy transition sectors, including renewable energy, e-
mobility, and circular economy sectors such as waste management and water. Moreover, IJF also aims
to promote investments by Japanese companies in India, including collaboration between Indian and
Japanese companies across sectors.

elements.label: para
elements.text: For additional information, please visit NIIF's website at www.niifindia.in

elements.label: para

elements.label: sec_1
elements.text: About EKA (Pinnacle Mobility Solutions Pvt. Ltd.):

elements.label: para
elements.text:
EKA (Pinnacle Mobility Solutions Pvt. Ltd.), with Mitsui & Co., Ltd. (Japan) & VDI Groep (Netherlands) as

equity partners, is an automotive & technology company creating a new global CV electric mobility

paradigm. EKA’s vision is to lead the world to a sustainable future by creating an ecosystem for

Environment-Conscious Mobility.

elements.label: fig
elements.text: ![Figure](figures/3PG_PLAIN TEXT_NIIFs-India-Japan-Fund-invests-INR-500-cr-US-57-mn-in-EKA-Mobility_page_003_figure_000.png)

elements.label: fig
elements.text: ![Figure](figures/3PG_PLAIN TEXT_NIIFs-India-Japan-Fund-invests-INR-500-cr-US-57-mn-in-EKA-Mobility_page_003_figure_001.png)

elements.label: para
elements.text:
With an industry-leading team, cutting-edge technology, modular designs, and lean manufacturing
processes, EKA’s mission is to bring reliable and efficient mobility solutions to the masses. EKA’s
sharable technologies and low-investment production processes enable the lowest TCO and
democratize electric vehicles for mass adaptation.

elements.label: para
elements.text: To know more about Eka Mobility, kindly visit:
